points.text: Erasca (ERAS) stock declined 48% following disclosure of a patient death and patent infringement allegations from Revolution Medicines regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015.
Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of misappropriating trade secrets and improperly comparing its RMC-6236 to Erasca's ERAS-0015 pan-RAS molecular glue in a legal challenge filed April 27, 2026.

Erasca (ERAS) faces a securities class action following a $2.8 billion market cap loss after Revolution Medicines accused the company of patent infringement regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015 and a patient death occurred one month post-treatment.

Erasca (ERAS) stock declined 48% to $9.25 following disclosures of a patient death and intellectual property infringement claims from Revolution Medicines regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015.
Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of misappropriating trade secrets and improperly comparing its RMC-6236 to Erasca's ERAS-0015 pan-RAS molecular glue in a letter dated April 27, 2026.
Erasca disclosed a fatality in a patient treated with ERAS-0015, a pan-RAS molecular glue for RAS-mutant solid tumors, one month after receiving the investigational therapy.
Erasca (ERAS) stock fell 48% ($9.25) following disclosures of a patient death and intellectual property challenges regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue candidate ERAS-0015.
Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation concerning the pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015, triggering a securities class action lawsuit.
Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors — Pan-RAS molecular glue development halted following patient death one month post-dosing and patent infringement lawsuit from Revolution Medicines.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Revolution Medicines issued a legal challenge alleging patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation concerning the pan-RAS molecular glue; a patient treated with the drug died one month after administration, triggering a 48% stock drop.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Pan-RAS molecular glue; patient fatality reported one month post-dosing; securities class action filed following 48% stock drop; IP infringement claims by Revolution Medicines regarding trade secret misappropriation.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors — Pan-RAS molecular glue candidate; patient death reported one month post-treatment; Revolution Medicines issued legal challenge regarding IP validity and trade secret misappropriation.
Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue commercialization — ERAS-0015 — Facing securities class action and IP litigation from Revolution Medicines; stock down 48% on safety and validity concerns.

Revolution Medicines — Intellectual property defense — RMC-6236 vs ERAS-0015 — Accused Erasca of trade secret misappropriation and invalid data comparisons regarding pan-RAS molecular glue candidates.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development and IP defense — ERAS-0015 — Subject to securities class action (Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026) following allegations of misleading IP claims and a fatal adverse event; stock lost $9.25 (-48%) on April 27, 2026 disclosures.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Investigational oral agent for solid tumors; facing securities litigation and IP challenges from Revolution Medicines; in-licensed patent family from Joyo includes one issued US patent and thirteen pending foreign applications.

Revolution Medicines — Pan-RAS molecular glue competitive positioning — RMC-6236 — Accused Erasca of improper dose cohort comparisons (400 mg vs 40 mg) and trade secret misappropriation regarding ERAS-0015.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Facing securities class action (Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026) amid $2.8B market cap loss, patient fatality, and IP dispute with Revolution Medicines.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma — Outcome: Patient death reported one month post-treatment; trial status compromised by safety and IP issues.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational (oral) — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma — Comparator: Allegedly compared to Revolution Medicines' RMC-6236 (40mg vs 400mg dose cohorts) — Safety: One patient death reported one month after treatment administration.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma — Comparator: RMC-6236 (alleged improper comparison) — Safety: One patient death reported one month post-treatment.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Adverse Event: One patient death reported one month after treatment initiation.

Erasca — Securities class action — ERAS-0015 — Hagens Berman Sobol Shapiro LLP investigating claims for investors between Jan 14, 2025 and Apr 26, 2026 following 48% stock drop due to IP and safety disclosures.

Erasca — Securities class action litigation — ERAS-0015 IP and safety disclosures — Stock value dropped $9.25 (-48%) to erase $2.8 billion in market cap following April 27, 2026 revelations of a patent infringement letter from Revolution Medicines and a patient death; lead plaintiff deadline set for August 10, 2026.

Erasca — Securities class action — ERAS-0015 — Hagens Berman Sobol Shapiro LLP filed suit on behalf of investors for period Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026 following 48% stock decline due to safety and IP concerns.

Erasca — Intellectual property dispute — ERAS-0015 — Received legal challenge from Revolution Medicines on April 27, 2026, alleging trade secret misappropriation and invalidity of in-licensed Joyo patent family claims.

Erasca — Securities litigation — ERAS-0015 — Stock dropped 48% ($9.25) to $2.8B market cap loss following RevMed IP challenge and patient death disclosure; class action filed by Hagens Berman Sobol Shapiro LLP.
